body.title: [in_progress] Vetinari escalates unresolved system alarms into Akien's feed / a Claude-readable channel
body.description:
Alarms that nobody looks at are just quieter logs. Vetinari is ALREADY the administrator ('reports to Akien when human decisions are required', 'Akien escalation when needed' — his device docstring). The escalation infra already exists but is ORPHANED: system_alarm_notifier.py (tmux nag, notify-once-per-new/reopened) has ZERO callers, and there's a web ALARMS PANEL. Wire alarm-escalation into Vetinari's periodic health rollup so that an unresolved/new/chronic system alarm reaches Akien's feed and/or a channel Claude reads — 'Vetinari reaches into my feed when there's an alarm he needs help to resolve, or reaches out to Claude' (Akien, 2026-07-02). This is the SINK for the sources in T-alarm-callsites-groundloop-db-install.

Shape: on Vetinari's sweep, read the open system_alarms; for each new/un-notified (or chronic high-count) alarm, post ONE escalation to Akien's feed (murderbot-feed metaphor: 'Vetinari Feed' / Public) and/or a Claude-readable channel, pointing at `uu alarms`; stamp it notified (reuse the notifier's mark_notified contract) so increments don't re-post; a reopened signature re-posts. Prefer wiring the existing system_alarm_notifier over a parallel path.

**Affected files:** unseen_university/devices/vetinari/device.py (sweep hook), unseen_university/system_alarm_notifier.py (wire it / generalize the tmux-only nag to also post a feed/channel message), the channel/feed post path (unseen_university.channel).
**Design rules:** fail-soft (an escalation-send failure never breaks the sweep or the alarm drop); calm-signals (one nudge per new/reopened alarm, NOT per dedup increment — no storm, no urgency=high in autonomous posts); external-state (Vetinari restarts freely); address-instances-not-classes (post to Akien, to=the instance).
**Scope boundary:** IN — Vetinari consuming open alarms and escalating unresolved ones to Akien's feed / a Claude channel, dedup-once. OUT — the alarm-drop mechanics (exist); the per-callsite sources (T-alarm-callsites-groundloop-db-install); the broader 'what is Vetinari's full admin mandate' role question (open, Akien to decide — this ticket is just the alarm-escalation slice of it).
**Test plan:** given one open un-notified alarm, Vetinari's sweep posts exactly one escalation to Akien's feed and stamps it; a 2nd sweep with the same (now-notified) alarm posts NONE; a reopened (previously-archived, recurring) signature posts again; an escalation-send failure leaves the sweep succeeding and the alarm un-stamped (so it retries).

## Pre-computed constraints (auto-stamped at add time)
[hard_block] ⛔ NO SQLITE. EVER. POSTGRES OR FLAT-FILE ONLY. See banner at top of this file. — applies: all (source: CLAUDE.md#hard-rules)
[hard_block] Log every state change and every interface crossing. State changes: ticket status — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
[error] No TheIgors imports. UnseenUniversity must be portable without TheIgors present. — applies: all (source: CLAUDE.md#hard-rules)
[error] No live keys or passwords in source. `.env` is gitignored. — applies: all (source: CLAUDE.md#hard-rules)
[error] `pip install -e .` must succeed at all times (even with empty stubs). — applies: all (source: CLAUDE.md#hard-rules)
[error] Canonical dev-process memory — one home, no exceptions. Every dev-process artifact — — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
[error] device.py and shim.py are the design center. Every component inherits from — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
[error] bus/ owns comms:// routing. Nothing outside bus/ speaks to IMAP directly. — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
[error] skeleton/ owns the MCP aggregator and flat-file registry. No Postgres dependency. — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
[error] devices/ contains one subdirectory per device; each is independently deployable. — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
[error] `~/.unseen_university/logs/<device>/<stream>/` is the canonical log hierarchy — — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
[error] Every ticket closes proven, or declares itself unproven — no discriminator. A ticket that — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
…and 39 more (lower-severity or capped) — query constraints_get for the full set
